_comment: Revised Bloom's Taxonomy (Anderson & Krathwohl, 2001). Six cognitive process dimensions with action verbs. Used by cognitive-rigor atom to classify objectives and assessment items. Source: public-domain summary of the published taxonomy.
source: Anderson, L.W. & Krathwohl, D.R. (2001). A Taxonomy for Learning, Teaching, and Assessing.

levels.name: Remember
levels.definition: Retrieve relevant knowledge from long-term memory.

levels.name: Understand
levels.definition: Construct meaning from instructional messages, including oral, written, and graphic communication.

levels.name: Apply
levels.definition: Carry out or use a procedure in a given situation.

levels.name: Analyze
levels.definition: Break material into constituent parts and determine how parts relate to one another and to an overall structure or purpose.

levels.name: Evaluate
levels.definition: Make judgments based on criteria and standards.

levels.name: Create
levels.definition: Put elements together to form a coherent or functional whole; reorganize elements into a new pattern or structure.

knowledge_dimensions.description: Basic elements students must know — terminology, specific details.
Interrelationships among basic elements — categories, principles, theories.
How to do something — methods, techniques, algorithms, criteria for use.
Knowledge of cognition and awareness of one's own cognition.
